sections_cited: Sections 194-A and 194-B of the Customs Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Constitution Petition No. D- 1118 of 2019 _________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge _________________________________________________________ Priority Case: 1. For hearing of CMA No.5150/2019 (stay) 2. For hearing of main case. 23.10.2024. Ms. Dilkhurram Shaheen, Advocate for Petitioner. Mr. Munawar Ali Memon, Advocate holds brief for Dr. Shahnawaz, Advocate for Respondent. Mr. Kashif Nazeer, Assistant Attorney General. ********* This is a petition seeking implementation of an order passed by the Customs Appellate Tribunal, at Karachi. It appears that vide order dated 09.8.2024 passed by the Hon’ble Supreme Court1, whereby it has been held the Customs Appellate Tribunal has the power to execute order(s) passed by it while exercising jurisdiction under Sections 194-A and 194-B of the Customs Act, 1969, and consequently, since an adequate remedy is provided by law, the writ jurisdiction of the High Court cannot be invoked for executing order(s) passed by the Tribunal. In view of such position, this Petition is dismissed as not maintainable with pending application, whereas the Petitioner is at liberty to seek appropriate remedy as observed in the above referred order of the Hon’ble Supreme Court of Pakistan. JUDGE JUDGE Farhan/PS 1 in Civil Petition No.3391/2024 [Khalid alias Muhammad Khalid and others v. Collector of Customs (Adjudication), Customs House, Lahore, etc.],
